fields.System.Title: FE App initial config and infra
Phase 1 Case Intake (POC)
fields.System.Description: This feature establishes the foundation of the Case Workflow in Phase 1 by validating that core intake steps, eligibility verification, treatment selection, and rule execution can function together as a single, coherent flow, while intentionally deferring clinical review, protocol logic, and advanced authorization behavior to later phases.

fields.System.Title: [V1.R2.]  Patient Clinical Information and Supporting Details
Auto-Determination Logic
fields.System.Description: Evaluates a submitted case to determine whether it qualifies for full system auto-determination based on OH auto-approval logic, payer drug configurations (AP/PDL),  and health plan business rules. This evaluation establishes whether the case can be completed without manual clinical or operational intervention.

fields.System.Title: Queues Management
fields.System.Description: Routes cases requiring manual review to the appropriate operational queue (CRS, Nursing, PharmD, MD) based on OncoHealth workqueue rules, clinical complexity, and payer-specific assignment logic, ensuring workload visibility and auditability.

fields.System.Title: Medical Drug Authorization Outbound (Health Plan Integration)
Tech Debt
fields.System.Description:
DAL

  • Introduce Unit of Work pattern (Remove SaveChangesAsync() calls from repository methods) (done)

  • Add .AsNoTracking() to all read-only queries

  • Configure a global query filter for ISoftDelete entities. Remove manual soft-delete checks from repository methods

  • Remove Mapper dependency from repository layer. Mapping should be done outside repository level

  • Remove IDisposable from repository base class (done)

  • Convert RepositoryBase into an abstract base class and refactor entity-specific repositories (e.g., CaseRepository) to inherit from it, removing redundant context fields and keeping only entity-specific logic where needed

  • Missing CancellationToken Propagation
  • Timezone issue (Should use DateTime.UtcNow for consistency with other code that uses UTC)
  • Repositories storing _ctx again are unnecessary
  • Fragile casting chain in mappers return (T)(IDto<Case>)new CaseDTO
  • In-memory filtering after loading all data (check all places)
  • Use AddAsync() only when using value generators that access the database, 99% need to use Add() since REAL async happens in await _ctx.SaveChangesAsync() (done)
  • Fix namespaces in DAL.Model. Screenshot attached 
  • agree on using fluent API or data annotation on entity models. See example of [Foreing Key] vs HasForeignKey(x => x.Version) OnModelCreating. In EF fluent Api has priority #1

fields.System.Title: Data Migration Pipeline - Backfill & Incremental Notebooks
fields.System.Description:
We need two separate notebooks/jobs to handle data migration:

1. Backfill Notebook (one-off, reusable) Handles the initial historical data load. Instead of reading the entire source dataset at once (which may be risky given unknown production data volumes), it processes data day-by-day in batch mode. It starts from the earliest available record date, migrates that full day, then moves to the next day, and so on. This approach is safer for large datasets and avoids memory issues.

This notebook is designed as a one-off migration but is reusable, if we ever need to re-migrate specific date ranges in the future (eg, gap filling), we can run it again with custom start/end parameters.

2. Incremental Notebook (nightly scheduled) Handles ongoing daily data migration. Uses a watermark table to track the last successfully migrated date and resumes from that point. This runs on a nightly batch schedule and only processes new/recent data (eg, yesterday's records).

Why this approach: We currently only have access to dev/test environments with a small dataset. Production may contain a significantly larger volume of data. Processing the entire history in a single run is risky without visibility into prod data volumes. The day-by-day backfill approach lets us migrate safely regardless of data size.

Acceptance Criteria

  • Backfill notebook created -  accepts start date and end date parameters
  • Backfill notebook processes data one day at a time in a loop, committing each day before moving to the next
  • Backfill notebook logs progress (current date being processed, record count per day)
  • Backfill notebook is idempotent - re-running for the same date does not create duplicates
  • Incremental notebook created - reads the last migrated date from a watermark table and processes from that point
  • Watermark table created to store: table name, last migrated date, last run timestamp, status
  • Incremental notebook updates the watermark table after each successful run
  • Incremental notebook configured as a nightly scheduled Job
  • Both notebooks integrate with the audit logging framework
  • Tested in dev/test environment before production deployment

Stories:

  1. Design and create watermark table schema
  2. Build backfill notebook with date range parameters and day-by-day processing loop
  3. Build incremental notebook with watermark-based resume logic
  4. Ensure idempotency on both notebooks (handle re-runs without duplicates)
  5. Integrate audit logging into both notebooks
  6. Create Databricks Job for nightly incremental schedule
  7. Test backfill with dev/test data
  8. Write documentation (usage, parameters, re-run instructions)
